textDisplay: Ex-machina, but a lot more…

I saw this movie during Mystery Movie Monday at my local theater, and I went in completely blind—it blew me away! I had no idea about any of the twists, which made the experience even more thrilling. My girlfriend thought we were walking into a romcom, so she was definitely surprised. Overall, an incredible movie and an unforgettable experience!

I saw an early screening with the director. He said in a panel that the first teaser wasn’t generating enough buzz, so when it was time to make this trailer, when in his own words, “you’re competing with millions of other movies coming out on streaming,” you’ve no choice but to spoil some things. He REALLY didn’t want to throw in the robot twist, but he acknowledged that we don’t live in a world where you can go into a movie without knowing anything about it anymore.

A lot of people are saying they were disapointed seeing the robot twist is revealed here and not blind, but honestly that has made me much more interested in the hook of the film! The trailer I saw in the theaters was fun in how unsettling it was, but seemed like it was a bit too uncomfortable knowing it was a person with the hand burning scene paired with the sexual violence, just felt too either real (with the SA) or uncanny with the hand burning. However with the robot hook, it makes not only a bit of dissonance, but at the same time adds to how people would treat her so horrifical and play with how misogyny  will let you see women as objects despite they are clearly people. Someone made to fit a mold and becoming aware of that shows us what the conflict is. Like it makes me think of a trailer like Abigale, where like when watching it yeah I would have wished I didn't know it was a vampire movie because it feels like it's a fun twist in the movie, but also if I didn't know the pitch I wouldn't have checked it out. It's a shame people feel like this told them the whole movie, but this just feels like a taste of the themes to expect and it really has made me excited and won over.

I like this trailer

If they were going to tell us this & act like it's the basic premise of the movie, then they should've showed it in the teaser somehow, which I like that they didn't do considering it was the mystery & not knowing that got me interested in the first place!

Sometimes you might like to see glimpses of cool action & funny lines to get you in the theater so you can hopefully see more of that, but I also like the unknown, I like going into a movie as blind as possible, & if this film treats this as a twist that we weren't supposed to know going in, then it's a bad trailer that ruins the enjoyment of what was supposed to be a surprise to shake up this story's status quo & makes it so people don't want to see what they already know.

It's honestly baffling that I might need to explain this to people who make & promote movies as their actual job, but you know what, I'm willing to be proven wrong with the final product that I'll see with my friends, who I'm going to warn that they do NOT watch this trailer beforehand, so if it's like how I think it is, at least they can enjoy it in a way I now can't.
